advantages: Immutable, so they can be hashed, cached and shared safely.
Rich, well-tested standard library of methods (split, join, replace, format).
Native Unicode support — no surprises with international text or emoji.
f-strings make formatting and interpolation fast and readable.
disadvantages: Immutability means every 'edit' allocates and copies — costly without join().
Indexing counts code points, not bytes; UTF-8 byte length and character length differ.
Easy to mix up `str` and `bytes` at I/O boundaries.
Character count is not a token count — costs and context limits need a tokenizer.
